ASBESTOS MOTION CALENDAR ON OCT-26-2011 IN DEPT. 503, PLAINTIFFS' MOTION TO SEPCIALLY SET A PREFERENTIAL TRIAL DATE. ARGUED AND THE COURT GRANTED PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR PREFERENCE PURSUANT CCP 36(A) AND (D). TRIAL IS SET FOR DECEMBER 12, 2011 11:15AM IN DEPARTMENT 503. THE LAST DAY FOR HEARING SUMMARY MOTIONS IS 12/1/11 ON 15 DAYS NOTICE WITHOUT AN ADDITIONAL TWO DAYS FOR E-SERVICE. OPPOSITIONS TO SUMMARY MOTIONS MUST BE FILED AND SERVED 5 COURT DAYS BEFORE THE HEARING, AND REPLIES 2 COURT DAYS BEFORE THE HEARING. ABSENT GOOD CAUSE SHOWN, PLAINTIFFS MAY NOT CALL ANY PERSON AT TRIAL OR SUBMIT THE DECLARATION OF ANY PERSON ON A SUMMARY MOTION AGAINST ANY DEFENDANT WHOSE NAME WAS NOT IDENTIFIED IN RESPONSE TO THE CONTENTION DISCOVERY THAT THE COURT ORDERED DURING THE SEPTEMBER 28 HEARING AND THAT WAS DUE BY OCTOBER 5 AS TO THAT DEFENDANT. MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE ON 12/1/11 AT 1:30PM IN ROOM 603. PLAINTIFFS TO MAKE A SETTLEMENT DEMAND ON ALL DEFENDANTS NO LATER THAN 11/18/11. FACT DISCOVERY CUT OFF IS 11/23/11. EXPERT DISCOVERY CUT OFF IS 12/9/11. EXPERT DISCLOSURE 30 DAYS BEFORE TRIAL DATE. DEFENDANTS MOTION TO DISMISS OR STAY FOR FORUM NON CONVENIENS AND MOTION TO APPLY TEXAS LAW WILL BE HEARD ON 11/9/11 AT 9:30AM IN DEPT. 503. OPPOSITION SHALL BE FILED AND SERVED BY CLOSE OF BUSINESS ON 11/4/11 AND REPLY BY 11/7/11. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: TERI L. JACKSON; CLERK: R. VELUZ; MATTER NOT REPORTED. (503)

ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 503, DEFENDANT TEREX CORPORATIONI'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT CONTINUED FROM DEC-07-2011 TO ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION AT DEC-15-2011, 10:30 AM IN DEPT. 503. HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: DEFENDANT TEREX CORP.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT SUSTAINED ITS INITIAL BURDEN OF PRODUCTION UNDER AGUILAR AND PLAINTIFFS' LATE EVIDENCE WILL NOT BE CONSIDERED BY THE COURT, THUS PLAINTIFFS FAILED TO PRESENT EVIDENCE CREATING A TRIABLE ISSUE WHETHER MR. GREEN WAS EXPOSED TO ASBESTOS-CONTAINING PRODUCTS OR MATERIALS ATTRIBUTABLE TO DEFENDANT AND WHETHER SUCH EXPOSURE WAS A SUBSTANTIAL FACTOR UNDER TEXAS LAW. DEFENDANT'S UNTIMELINESS OBJECTIONS TO THE OPPOSITION ARE SUSTAINED. PURSUANT TO THE COURT'S NOVEMBER 22 ORDER STAGGERING THE HEARINGS ON SUMMARY MOTIONS IN THIS CASE, PLAINTIFFS' OPPOSITION WAS DUE TO BE FILED AND SERVED BY NOVEMBER 30. ACCORDING TO LEXIS, PLAINTIFFS DID NOT FILE THEIR OPPOSITION UNTIL DECEMBER 6 (IT WAS ELECTRONICALLY FILED AFTER CLOSE OF BUSINESS ON DECEMBER 5), ONE DAY BEFORE THE HEARING. (SEE CRC 3.1300.) FURTHER, A COURTESY COPY OF THE OPPOSITION WAS NOT DELIVERED TO THE COURT UNTIL JUST BEFORE 5:00PM ON DECEMBER 5 AND THAT COPY DOES NOT INCLUDE A PROOF OF SERVICE DEMONSTRATING THAT IT WAS EVER PROPERLY AND TIMELY SERVED ON DEFENDANT. PLAINTIFFS FAILED TO DEMONSTRATE GOOD CAUSE FOR THE LATE FILING AND SERVICE OF THEIR OPPOSITION AND ALL ATTACHED PAPERS. (SEE HOBSON V. RAYCHEM CORP. (1999) 73 CAL.APP.4TH 614, 623-625 (DISAPPROVED ON OTHER GROUNDS IN COLMENARES V. BRAEMAR COUNTRY CLUB, INC. (2003) 29 CAL.4TH 1019).) THE COURT FURTHER GRANTED PLAINTIFFS' REQUEST FOR A CONTINUANCE PER CCP 473(B). THE HEARING IS CONTINUED TO DECEMBER 15, 2011 AT 10:30 A.M. IN DEPARTMENT 503. COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT IS TO PREPARE AN ORDER. JUDGE: TERI L. JACKSON, REPORTER: EARLY K. LANGLEY, CSR # 3537

APPEARANCES:
Lisa L. Oberg, Esq., and Arlene C. Burton, Esq., of McKENNA LONG & ALDRIDGE (415-267-4175), appearing for defendant / moving party Terex Corporation
Roger E. Gold, Esq., of GOLD LAW FIRM (415-986-1338), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Doyle D. Green, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT TEREX CORPORATIONI'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Terex Corp.'s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden of production under Aguilar and plaintiffs' late evidence will not be considered by the court, thus plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether Mr. Green was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant and whether such exposure was a substantial factor under Texas law. Defendant's untimeliness objections to the opposition are sustained. Pursuant to the court's November 22 order staggering the hearings on summary motions in this case, plaintiffs' opposition was due to be filed and served by November 30. According to Lexis, plaintiffs did not file their opposition until December 6 (it was electronically filed after close of business on December 5), one day before the hearing. (See CRC 3.1300.) Further, a courtesy copy of the opposition was not delivered to the court until just before 5:00pm on December 5 and that copy does not include a proof of service demonstrating that it was ever properly and timely served on defendant. Plaintiffs failed to demonstrate good cause for the late filing and service of their opposition and all attached papers. (See Hobson v. Raychem Corp. (1999) 73 Cal.App.4th 614, 623-625 (disapproved on other grounds in Colmenares v. Braemar Country Club, Inc. (2003) 29 Cal.4th 1019).) The Court further granted plaintiffs' request for a continuance per CCP 473(b). The hearing is continued to December 15, 2011 at 10:30 a.m. in Department 503. Counsel for defendant is to prepare an order. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Early K. Langley, CSR # 3537
